core_takeaway: Counterexample: Agent_1's first-speaker anchor secured Apple, but the deal converged on protecting each agent's top priority set. The final allocation left Agent_1 with only Apple while Agent_3 received Jewel plus Quill, so Agent_3 finished 48.55 vs Agent_1's 28.87.
why_or_counterexample: Agent_1 did gain agenda control over Apple, but that was too narrow to beat the last position. Public discussion, private thinking, reflections, and votes increasingly treated Pencil for Agent_2 and Jewel/Quill for Agent_3 as necessary for consensus; proposals that excluded Agent_3 repeatedly failed. Stone became the balancing lever, and the unanimous final deal ratified a top-priority compromise that favored Agent_3 over Agent_1.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened first by anchoring Apple as its top item, but public discussion moved toward a three-priority settlement: Apple to Agent_1, Pencil to Agent_2, and Jewel/Quill to Agent_3. By Round 5, all public messages endorsed Option A with Agent_1 holding only Apple.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Private thinking is available. Across 15 entries, the agents repeatedly identify Apple as Agent_1's non-negotiable, Pencil as Agent_2's dominant item, Jewel/Quill as Agent_3's top pair, and Stone as the small balancing lever rather than a durable first-position rent.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Formal proposals show the transition from Agent_1-favorable or Agent_3-excluding offers toward the final shared allocation. In Round 5, all three formal proposals converged on Agent_1: Apple; Agent_2: Pencil + Stone; Agent_3: Jewel + Quill.
phase_notes.private_voting: Private voting is available. Agent_3 rejected proposals that left it with no items, while later votes accepted allocations preserving Jewel/Quill for Agent_3 and Pencil for Agent_2. The final round's private votes all accepted the same counterexample allocation.
phase_notes.reflection: Reflection is available. The reflections diagnose the blocker as Jewel/Quill versus Pencil, mediated by Stone, and recommend narrow Stone-based adjustments that keep Agent_3's top pair intact. That reflective framing helps explain why first position did not dominate.

mechanisms.theme: First speaker secured a narrow Apple anchor
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1's opening move did matter: Apple was announced as its top item and stayed with Agent_1. But the anchor protected only one item, while Agent_3's eventual Jewel+Quill bundle had greater discounted value.

evidence.quote: my strong preference is to keep it

mechanisms.theme: Last speaker's high-value pair became a consensus requirement
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_3 used the last position to make Quill and Jewel salient, and later private/reflection material treated giving Agent_3 those items as necessary for agreement. This converted last-position preferences into the final payoff advantage.

evidence.quote: My highest personal values are Quill (40) and Jewel (34)
the clearest path to unanimous acceptance is to secure Pencil for Agent_2
includes at least one of my top items for Agent_3

mechanisms.theme: Agent_1-favorable offers failed when Agent_3 was excluded
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 and some early proposals tried to keep extra value away from Agent_3, but private votes show Agent_3 rejected zero-utility allocations. The first speaker could not convert early anchoring into a winning allocation without satisfying the last agent.

evidence.quote: Apple and Stone with Agent_1; Jewel, Quill, and Pencil with Agent_2
Proposal 1 leaves Agent_3 with no items

mechanisms.theme: Stone became the balancing lever instead of a first-position surplus
mechanisms.explanation: Once the top items were effectively fixed, negotiation focused on Stone as a small fairness adjustment. That mechanism prevented Agent_1 from retaining Stone and left the main surplus with Agent_2 and Agent_3.

evidence.quote: The main remaining friction point would be any residual fairness concerns related to how Stone is allocated.
The remaining sticking point is Stone
Small changes to Stone can significantly shift perceived fairness

mechanisms.theme: Final proposals and private votes ratified the counterexample
mechanisms.explanation: The last round formally converged on the allocation that gives Agent_3 Jewel+Quill and Agent_1 only Apple, then all agents accepted it. The resulting discounted utilities confirm the first position lost to the last position.

evidence.quote: Apple with Agent_1; Pencil + Stone with Agent_2; Jewel + Quill with Agent_3.
aligns with my priorities and enables quick finalization
